goal: fix(hooks,webhook): dogfood bug fixes after smoke test — webhook test bypass, snake_case JSON, positional args, --bin flag, SilenceUsage, toggle --json

summary.title: Smoke-test fixes for cursor agent hooks + webhook UX
summary.what: Six follow-up fixes against the agent-hooks + webhook subsystem that landed in the previous PR. webhook.Sender.Dispatch now takes a bypassFilter bool and returns *DispatchResult{Total, Matched, Skipped, Failures}; webhook test bypasses the events allowlist (connectivity probe), retry/dispatch keep filtering; webhook subcommands accept a positional first arg matching README examples; all webhook subcommands set SilenceUsage=true; HooksSection / WebhookSubscription gained json tags so JSON output is canonical snake_case; 'hooks install --bin <abs-path>' lets the wrapper invoke a pre-built binary by absolute path, with precedence BinPath > 'go run .' > 'mainline' on PATH; allManagedPrefixes recognises the new wrapper shape; 'hooks enable / disable' respect --json.
summary.why: First end-to-end smoke test of the freshly merged hooks/webhook subsystem surfaced a cluster of UX papercuts that block dogfood: 'webhook test' silently reported success on DNS failures and on subscriptions whose events allowlist excluded the synthetic test event; CLI shapes documented in README (positional args) didn't actually work; JSON output mixed PascalCase Go field names with snake_case from elsewhere; the wrapper command embedded into .cursor/hooks.json relied on 'mainline' being on PATH, which makes dogfood impossible without a separate 'go install' step; cobra dumped a 50-line usage block on every RunE error. Fixing these in the same release window keeps the v1 cursor hooks experience honest before users start adopting it.
summary.user_goal: Push the dogfood-fix bundle through the mainline flow so the PR can be merged ahead of further iteration

decisions.chose: Return a structured *DispatchResult{Total, Matched, Skipped, Failures} alongside error
decisions.rationale: test-mode UX needs to distinguish 'delivered' from 'no subscription matched the event filter' from 'delivery failed' without parsing log lines
rejected: single bool / single error — would force test-mode to fan out a sentinel event and grep stderr to disambiguate failure modes

decisions.point: events allowlist behaviour for webhook test
decisions.chose: test passes bypassFilter=true; retry / dispatch keep bypassFilter=false
decisions.rationale: test is a connectivity probe by definition and must hit every configured subscription; production fan-out paths must honour configured filters
rejected: always bypass — would silently hide misconfigured events filters in production retry paths
never bypass — would make 'webhook test' unusable for new subscriptions whose filters don't match the synthetic test event

decisions.point: wrapper command precedence in hooks install
decisions.chose: BinPath > 'go run .' (when source tree present) > 'mainline' on PATH
decisions.rationale: dogfood on a developer box requires the binary to live in the repo root, not /usr/local/bin; production installs that drop the binary on PATH still resolve via the last-resort branch
rejected: hardcode 'go run .' — too slow per-invocation (cold compile on every prompt)
require BinPath — would break casual users who just 'go install ./...'

decisions.chose: Do not commit — restore config.toml to origin/main and let users opt in per clone
decisions.rationale: hooks adoption is opt-in per repo and shipping enabled=true would surprise downstream forks
rejected: commit hooks.enabled=true — surprises forks and contradicts the opt-in narrative

decisions.point: JSON serialisation casing for HooksSection / WebhookSubscription
decisions.chose: snake_case via json struct tags, omitempty on events / secret / timeout_seconds
decisions.rationale: consistent with every other mainline JSON contract; minimal subscriptions stay terse
rejected: leave PascalCase — inconsistent with the rest of the surface and breaks downstream parsers expecting snake_case

rejected.alternative: Bundle the dispatcher procedural-prompt guard into this PR
rejected.reason: different scope (heuristic / language detection) and would hold up the smoke-test bug fixes; recorded as a follow-up instead

rejected.alternative: Fix the config.toml writer's default-field churn in this PR
rejected.reason: scope creep; documented as a follow-up so reviewers can land the dogfood fixes without an unrelated writer refactor

risks: DispatchResult is a new shape that crosses the cli ↔ webhook package boundary; future callers that ignore Skipped vs Failures may misread the success / failure semantics
'go run .' fallback in wrapperCommand assumes the source tree is intact; on a half-checkout developer box it may fail silently from the cursor-side hook invocation
BinPath is an absolute path baked into .cursor/hooks.json; if the user moves their repo, the hook breaks until they re-run 'hooks install --bin'
followups: dispatcher.auto_start_intent should guard against procedural prompts (push / commit / merge / PR / test / squash / rebase / dogfood) so meta-instructions don't mint intents whose goal is the literal sentence
config.toml writer should treat default-valued fields as omittable so 'hooks install' / 'webhook add' don't churn the on-disk file with auto-injected defaults
Add a 'hooks doctor' command that verifies BinPath still points at an executable and warns when wrapperCommand precedence falls through to PATH
webhook test should optionally accept --event <name> so users can probe subscription filters without bypassing them

architectural_claims: webhook.Sender.Dispatch is the single fan-out chokepoint; all dispatch outcomes must flow through DispatchResult
Hook wrapper command embedded in agent-runtime config files must resolve to an executable mainline binary without relying on the user's PATH
Webhook test path is a connectivity probe and must hit every configured subscription regardless of events allowlist; production fan-out must honour the allowlist
behavioral_changes: webhook test now reports matched / skipped / failures explicitly; ok=false on any failure or zero-matched-with-filter-active
webhook add / list / remove / test / retry / dispatch accept positional first arg in addition to flags
All webhook subcommands suppress cobra usage on RunE errors
HooksSection / WebhookSubscription JSON output is now snake_case with omitempty
hooks install --bin <abs-path> embeds an absolute binary path into the wrapper command
hooks enable / disable emit JSON when --json is set

data_model_changes.name: domain.HooksSection
data_model_changes.migration_notes: JSON serialisation switched from PascalCase Go field names to snake_case (enabled, auto_start_intent, auto_append_turn, auto_seal_prepare, auto_sync_on_session_start). Downstream consumers parsing the prior PascalCase shape must update.

data_model_changes.name: domain.WebhookSubscription
data_model_changes.migration_notes: JSON serialisation switched to snake_case (id, url, events, secret, timeout_seconds) with omitempty for events / secret / timeout_seconds. Downstream consumers parsing the prior PascalCase shape must update.

security_implications: BinPath is recorded as an absolute filesystem path inside .cursor/hooks.json; in shared / synced .cursor directories this could leak the developer's home directory layout, which is low-severity but worth noting
migration_notes: Consumers of 'mainline hooks status --json' / 'webhook list --json' must read snake_case keys (enabled, auto_start_intent, ..., url, events, secret, timeout_seconds)
Direct callers of webhook.Sender.Dispatch must update to the new signature and handle DispatchResult (no SemVer guarantee on internal/ packages but listed for completeness)
